title: Hudson man accused of beating grandmother to death with skateboard, also injuring father and sister
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Devin Dube, 29, allegedly murdered his grandmother and injured his sister and father with a skateboard in Hudson.
Dube was arrested 45 minutes after fleeing the scene, found hiding in a van on Tower Street.
Authorities say there is no threat to the public, and police have had prior contact with Dube.

title: Former Rev Esmir Bajraktarević scored winning penalty in World Cup playoff
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Esmir Bajraktarević scored the decisive penalty kick for Bosnia and Herzegovina against Italy, securing a World Cup spot.
Bosnia and Herzegovina's victory means Italy will miss a third consecutive World Cup since their last in 2014.
Iraq defeated Bolivia 2-1 to qualify for their first World Cup since 1986 and will face Norway at "Boston Stadium".

title: Medford schools to close on Good Friday due to staffing shortages
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Medford schools will close on April 3 due to staffing shortages from staff taking leave for religious observances.
Afterschool activities canceled, but district and school offices remain open; the end of school year schedule is adjusted.
Superintendent Galusi cites a high rate of staff absences; union planning to respond; school monitors attendance on religious holidays.

title: 5 restaurants we can’t wait to try in April
author: Jillian Dara
bullets: Avra Estiatorio, a modern Greek restaurant from New York City, will open in Back Bay, Boston in mid-April.
Dalia, a Spanish-inspired wood-fired restaurant in South Boston, will open on April 2 by the Broadway Restaurant Group.
La Tavernetta in East Boston, featuring coastal Italian cuisine, is set to open in early to mid-April.

title: Snowboarder sustains serious injuries in Cannon Mountain crash
author: John Waller
bullets: A 36-year-old snowboarder was seriously injured after hitting a tree outside boundaries at Cannon Mountain, NH.
The incident occurred Monday afternoon in a steep, rocky section not patrolled or maintained by the ski area.
Rescue involved ropes to extricate him; he was transported to a hospital after being rescued.

title: Boston police investigate stabbing near Northeastern University
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A person was stabbed near Northeastern University on St. Botolph Street Wednesday morning.
The injured individual, not linked to the university, was hospitalized after receiving aid from campus police.
Boston police are actively investigating and searching for the suspect, who fled the scene; no arrests made.

title: Local obituary: Rob Halpin, 59, owned Rudy’s Cafe in Somerville’s Teele Square
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Rob Halpin, owner of Rudy's Cafe in Somerville's Teele Square, passed away on March 26, 2026, at 59.
Raised in Somers, CT, Halpin graduated from Tufts University in 1988 and lived in the Greater Boston area.
A memorial service will be held at John Bryant Funeral Home in Wayland, MA, on April 4, 2026.

title: Local obituary: Cory Hartman, 43, N.H. dad to 3 daughters
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Cory Whitty Hartman, 43, of Hampton, N.H., passed away on March 28, 2026.
Hartman leaves behind his wife Ashley and three daughters, Lilah, Zoe, and Phoebe.
Visitation is scheduled for April 3 at Remick & Gendron Funeral Home in Hampton.

title: Amid slowdown, developers set to sell part of dormant Dorchester megaproject
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Accordia Partners is selling a 13.6-acre parcel of the Dorchester Bay City project at 2 Morrissey Blvd.
The site, purchased for $110 million in 2019, is marketed as a transformative development opportunity by brokerage firm Newmark.
The sale does not affect Accordia's agreement with UMass Boston, which could generate up to $235 million in revenue.

title: Here’s how much a substitute teacher makes in Lexington
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Substitute teacher Alice L., 62, earns $10,000–$12,000 annually working across Lexington, Belmont, and a third district.
Alice's per diem rate varies from $125 to $150, depending on the school district where she substitutes.
She lives in Burlington with monthly rent of $3,400, largely covered by her mother's finances and father's savings.
